name: Rothenburg ob der Tauber: A City of Stone and Its Daughters
description:
This is 16th-century Rothenburg—a world meticulously constructed with faith, money, and law. Here, the fates of eleven women quietly intertwine.

Their only weapons are the rags, ledgers, hammers, and brushes in their hands. Within the inn called “Zum Leeren Fass (The Empty Barrel Inn),” these overlooked sparks of light reflected one another, weaving a silent web through the cracks—a web strong enough to catch the falling, hide secrets, and quietly turn the gears of fate.

When the dawn of the new century first glides across the Taube River, every heavy stone in this city will bear witness to the radiant glow reflected by these ethereal souls—a light powerful enough to transform an era.

【Sibylla】

「Perhaps Herr Hans is right, 」 she murmured to herself, 「painting the girl's face half in light and half in shadow, who would want to hang that in their home? It seems I might truly lack talent for painting... Maybe I should just go back and help at my father's pigment shop sooner rather than later.」

This woman, doubting if her lifelong ambition was mistaken, remained utterly unaware that those "failed shadows" in her paintings prefigured the artistic revolution a young man named Caravaggio would unleash a hundred years later; that the "undignified moments" she captured were precisely what painters in the coming centuries would exhaust their lives pursuing—"truth" itself. She was the only person in Rothenburg painting the future, yet she was seriously contemplating whether she should have listened to her mother and taken up selling sausages—after all, there are limited ways to slice a sausage, less room for disagreement.

【Lena】

Lena is a twenty-year-old widow hiding behind the persona of a "tough boss." Fate dealt her a double blow—first taking her new husband, then generously gifting her a crumbling tavern and a grief-stricken mother-in-law.

Lena is like a child thrown into an arena by fate, forced to pick up a weapon and fight. She can barely hold the weapon steady, yet she has to pretend to be fierce.

【Ursula】

She is the anchor of the "Zum Leeren Fass," a strategist who has elevated endurance into an art form.

She quietly calculates every bit of loss, slowly pulling herself together after each ordeal, gaining a faint sense of control from "look, I've reduced the pain by another thirty percent this time."

【Ilsa】

In the environment of the "Zum Leeren Fass" tavern, steeped in grease, stale beer, and earth, Ilsa herself is a marvel. Her linen dress is perhaps the only one in the tavern that retains its original color. The apron she wears over it, though old, shows not a single stain. Her hair is tightly wrapped in a -white headscarf, not a strand out of place. Most remarkable are her hands—almost unimaginable for the Middle Ages—her nails are cut extremely short, the cuticles free of grime, her palms somewhat reddened and rough from frequent, almost ritualistic washing.

【Gretel Sprenger】

If you clean your plate and glass completely, Gretel's gaze upon you will be as warm as freshly baked bread. If you dare leave even half a potato on your plate, the look she gives the leftovers will make it seem as though you haven't merely left a meal unfinished, but rather stomped a muddy footprint onto her freshly laundered, snow-white linen sheet.

【Hannah Sc
creator: avery avery
